nodes.name: Sequence Transduction Models
nodes.description: The general class of neural network architectures designed to map input sequences to output sequences, which the Transformer aims to improve upon.

nodes.name: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN)
nodes.description: The previously dominant architecture for sequence modeling, which processes data sequentially and is being replaced by the Transformer.

nodes.name: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN)
nodes.description: An alternative architecture to RNNs for sequence modeling that allows for some parallelization but still lacks the global receptive field of self-attention.

nodes.name: Encoder-Decoder Architecture
nodes.description: The standard structural framework for sequence-to-sequence tasks consisting of an encoder that processes the input and a decoder that generates the output.

nodes.name: Transformer
nodes.description: The proposed novel network architecture that relies entirely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolution entirely.

nodes.name: Self-Attention
nodes.description: An attention mechanism where the queries, keys, and values all originate from the same sequence (the input or previous decoder layer), allowing the model to relate different positions within a sequence.

nodes.name: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
nodes.description: The specific attention function used in the Transformer that computes the dot product of the query and keys, scales by the inverse square root of the dimension, and applies softmax.

nodes.name: Multi-Head Attention
nodes.description: A mechanism that allows the model to jointly attend to information from different representation subspaces at different positions by running multiple attention functions in parallel.

nodes.name: Position-wise Feed-Forward Networks
nodes.description: Fully connected feed-forward networks applied to each position separately and identically, consisting of two linear transformations with a ReLU activation in between.

nodes.name: Positional Encoding
nodes.description: Information injected into the model to compensate for the lack of recurrence and convolution, using sine and cosine functions of different frequencies to indicate token position.

nodes.name: Residual Connections
nodes.description: Connections employed around each of the sub-layers in the encoder and decoder stacks to facilitate the training of deep networks.

nodes.name: Layer Normalization
nodes.description: A technique used to stabilize the hidden state dynamics in deep networks, applied after the residual connections in the Transformer.

nodes.name: Encoder-Decoder Attention
nodes.description: The attention layer in the decoder where the queries come from the previous decoder layer and the keys and values come from the output of the encoder stack.

nodes.name: Additive Attention
nodes.description: An alternative attention function that computes compatibility using a feed-forward network with a single hidden layer, referenced as a comparison to dot-product attention.

nodes.name: Label Smoothing
nodes.description: A regularization technique used during training where the target probability is discounted and distributed to other tokens to improve model calibration and performance.

nodes.name: Adam Optimizer
nodes.description: The optimization algorithm used for training the Transformer, with specific settings for beta parameters and a custom learning rate schedule.

nodes.name: BLEU Score
nodes.description: The standard metric for evaluating machine translation quality, used to demonstrate the Transformer's state-of-the-art performance.
